description: Amidst a deep economic recession in 2015, the Rousseff administration proposed bringing back the CPMF (a tax on financial transactions) to balance the budget. Public opposition was intense due to previous corruption scandals.

key_events.description: Government announces need for new tax revenue to avoid deficit.
Major business federations publicly denounce the proposal.
Massive anti-government protests incorporate 'no new taxes' rhetoric.
Congressional leaders declare the bill dead on arrival.

calibration_notes: Exemplifies how low institutional trust dramatically hardens opposition to new tax proposals, regardless of fiscal necessity.
